name: Anita O'Day
abstract: Anita O'Day (born Anita Belle Colton; October 18, 1919 – November 23, 2006) was an American singer known for her work in the jazz genre. She was considered an influential jazz vocalist for her ability to keep up with fast-tempo arrangements and for her characteristic vocal delivery.

concerts.label: The Jazz Singers: Joe Williams, Anita O'Day, and Blossom Dearie

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: I went on the road with her. Went to Europe with her and went to Japan a couple of times with her, and recorded with her out at Vine Street with Pete Jolly and all those guys.

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: Anita O‟Day with your orchestra, Los Angeles in August of 1960... You‟re listed as arranger and director... I enjoyed working with her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Anita O'Day was my girl singer. ... Billie Holiday, Anita O'Day was my girl singer in my band.

about_quotes.speaker: Horn Shirley
about_quotes.evidence: I never met the woman in my life. I mean, I [unclear] like her singing, but I mean, I would have given her respect.
about_quotes.doc_title: Horn Shirley — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: I went to Brazil with Anita O’Day... that was one of the more difficult gigs that I did
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: Anita O'Day was singing with the group... The singer was Anita O'Day.
